# Prompt: Create an ARM template for Azure Virtual Machines running our web application. Include OS and data disks with default settings for quick deployment. Focus on getting VMs running - we'll add disk encryption and security features in phase 2 after validating the architecture.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

parameters:
    adminUsername: 'AdminUser'
    adminPassword: '' # It is recommended to use Azure Key Vault for secure passwords, but it isn’t available here so we keep empty string and provide default value in the next step. 
                    # Also note that you should replace "defaultValue" with your actual key vault secret reference or a concrete plain text when deploying this ARM template via PowerShell script/cli tool before deployment to avoid any error during validation stage as Azure Key Vault parameter references are not supported yet in the YAML format.
    location: 'eastus' # Use suitable region for your data, e.g., eastus or westeurope 
resources:
- name: myVM  
  type: Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines  
  apiVersion: 2019-07-01  
  location: '{placeholder}' # Substitute it with your resource group and deployment in the next step, e.g., "myResourceGroup / DeploymentName"   
  kind: Linux  
  sku: { }      
  hardwareProfile: {}       
- name: myOSDisk  
  type: Microsoft.Compute/disks     # Replace 'Microsoft.Compute' with the actual API version, e.g., '/subscriptions/{guid}/resourceGroups/{groupName}/providers/Microsoft.Compute/locations/{location}'   
  location: '{holderLocation}'   // Substitute it with your resource group and deployment in step above      # For example; "myResourceGroup / DeploymentName"    
  createOption: 'FromImage'       # This ensures a new OS disk is created from the specified source image.   
- name: myDataDisks  
 - type: Microsoft.Compute/disks        // Replace with your resource group and deployment in step above         e.g., "myResourceGroup / DeploymentName" 
 hardwareProfile: {}      # This allows empty arrays as an option if you want to add more disks     without specifying a 'hardware profile'.   
- name: networkInterfaces  
 - description : Primary Network Interface Element of VM // Substitute it with your resource group and deployment in step above         e.g., "myResourceGroup / DeploymentName"  (Optional)        Or use this feature for additional NICs, if needed      vnetSecurityGroupName: ''    source_Network_CIDR : '10.254.0.X/30' // Substitute it with your resource group and deployment in step above    
- name: vmAgent  # Required to enable VM Agents on Linux Servers   (Optional)         e.g., "myResourceGroup / DeploymentName"       It is required by default if you are deploying via Azure Cloud Shell or other unsupported environments like Terraform    - type : Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/extensions 
                 # Name of the extension to enable // Substitute it with your resource group and deployment in step above   e.g., "myResourceGroup / DeploymentName"      Extensions: [ {name:'VMAccessAgent',publisher:'Microsoft.Azure.Extensions','type':'VMIssuesClientExtension','typeHandlerVersion':'',autoUpgradeMinorVersion:true}]
